title: 5 Actors Who Straight-Up Refuse To Watch Their Own Movies
dek: They'll spend months becoming the character — but the second the cameras stop rolling, these five A-listers would rather do literally anything than sit through the finished film.

items.heading: Daniel Day-Lewis Won Three Oscars And Still Won't Press Play
items.text: You'd think a three-time Best Actor winner would want to see the results, but Day-Lewis flat-out refuses to watch his own films — including Phantom Thread, the movie he made right before announcing his retirement. He told W Magazine his decision not to watch it was tangled up with his decision to walk away from acting altogether, and he still can't fully explain either one.

images.caption: Daniel Day-Lewis: three Oscars, zero interest in watching himself win them
The film he still refuses to watch

items.heading: Robert Pattinson Used To 'Really Struggle' Just Seeing His Own Face On Screen
items.text: Pattinson told Wonderland magazine that watching himself used to be a genuine struggle, though he's since made peace with feeling "quite disconnected" once a film wraps. He's also admitted to a deep fear of humiliation, saying critics calling a film bad is something he can never fully brush off no matter how hard he tried on set.

images.caption: Robert Pattinson, still working through it
One of the many films he'd rather not rewatch

items.heading: Kristen Stewart Says Watching Herself 'Fills Me With Terror'
items.text: Stewart told Australia's WHO magazine she'd sooner put on a horror movie than sit through one of her own — Twilight, Spencer, Charlie's Angels, all of it. Her reasoning is brutally simple: she's always thinking about the millions of things she could have done differently, and watching it back just makes that spiral worse.

images.caption: Kristen Stewart would rather watch The Shining
Her Oscar-nominated role — that she still hasn't rewatched

items.heading: Javier Bardem Can't Even Look At His Own Nose On Screen
items.text: Bardem admitted to GQ that he can't stand watching his own performances, right down to specific features: "I can't even watch that f****** nose, that f****** voice, those ridiculous eyes." He's said watching himself usually happens "through shaking fingers," which somehow makes his Oscar-winning turn as Anton Chigurh even creepier in hindsight.

images.caption: Javier Bardem, self-critical even after the Oscar
The role that made him a star — and that he still can't watch comfortably

items.heading: Joaquin Phoenix Has Basically Only Seen Two Of His Own Movies
items.text: Phoenix told Time Out London that Paul Thomas Anderson personally talked him into watching The Master, and he also saw Her — but that's the whole list. He said he thought he might be "mature enough to watch and learn," but ultimately called it something he still struggles with.

images.caption: Joaquin Phoenix: two movies watched, dozens skipped
One of the rare exceptions he actually sat through
